# Compact Smart Contract Development Guide

## 1. What is Compact?

Compact is Midnight's domain-specific, strongly typed, statically typed, and bounded smart contract language, designed to be used alongside TypeScript. It compiles to JavaScript (and TypeScript definition files) as well as zero-knowledge (ZK) circuits. [[Compact overview](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#overview)]

Each Compact contract has three components:
- A **replicated public ledger** component (on-chain state)
- A **zero-knowledge circuit** component (proves valid execution without revealing private data)
- A **local off-chain** component (arbitrary code in TypeScript) [[Compact overview](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#overview)]

---

## 2. Installation

Install the Compact devtools and toolchain:

```bash
# Install devtools
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -LsSf \
  https://github.com/midnightntwrk/compact/releases/latest/download/compact-installer.sh | sh

# Add to PATH
source $HOME/.local/bin/env

# Install latest toolchain
compact update

# Verify
compact compile --version
```

[[installation guide](https://docs.midnight.network/getting-started/installation)]

---

## 3. Contract Structure

A Compact program can contain:
- `pragma` — language version declaration
- `import` — imports from modules or the standard library
- `enum` / `struct` — custom type definitions
- `ledger` — public on-chain state declarations
- `witness` — callback functions provided by the TypeScript DApp (private data)
- `constructor` — runs once on deployment to initialize state
- `circuit` — the operational functions of the contract

[[programs reference](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#programs)]

### Minimal Example

```compact
pragma language_version >= 0.22;

import CompactStandardLibrary;

export ledger round: Counter;

export circuit increment(): [] {
  round.increment(1);
}
```

[[counter tutorial](https://docs.midnight.network/tutorials/counter/smart-contract#write-the-smart-contract)]

---

## 4. Language Version Pragma

Always declare the language version at the top of your contract:

```compact
pragma language_version >= 0.22;
```

This locks your contract to a minimum Compact version and prevents breaking changes from future compiler versions. [[bboard tutorial](https://docs.midnight.network/tutorials/bboard/smart-contract#write-the-smart-contract)]

---

## 5. Standard Library

Import with:

```compact
import CompactStandardLibrary;
```

Key types and circuits provided: [[standard library](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/standard-library)]

| Category | Items |
|---|---|
| Common types | `Maybe<T>`, `Either<A,B>`, `ContractAddress`, `UserAddress` |
| Hashing | `transientHash`, `transientCommit`, `persistentHash`, `persistentCommit` |
| Elliptic curve | `ecAdd`, `ecMul`, `ecMulGenerator`, `hashToCurve` |
| Merkle tree | `merkleTreePathRoot`, `merkleTreePathRootNoLeafHash` |
| Coin management | `tokenType`, `nativeToken`, `sendShielded`, `receiveShielded`, `mintShieldedToken` |
| Optional values | `some<T>(value)`, `none<T>()` |

---

## 6. Primitive Types

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `Boolean` | `true` or `false` |
| `Uint<n>` | Sized unsigned integer (up to *n* bits) |
| `Uint<m..n>` | Bounded unsigned integer in range [m, n) |
| `Field` | Unsigned integer up to the ZK field order |
| `Bytes<n>` | Byte vector of length *n* |
| `Vector<n, T>` | Tuple of *n* values of type *T* |
| `[T1, T2, ...]` | Heterogeneous tuple |
| `Opaque<'string'>` | Opaque value (manipulable in witnesses, hashed in circuits) |

[[primitive types](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#primitive-types)]

---

## 7. Program-Defined Types

### Enumerations

```compact
export enum AuctionState {
  CLOSED,
  OPEN
}
```

[[enum types](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#enumeration-types)]

### Structures

```compact
struct NumberAnd<T> {
  num: Uint<32>;
  item: T
}
```

Structures are **nominal** (same name + same fields = same type). They cannot be recursive. [[struct types](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#structure-types)]

---

## 8. Ledger State (Public On-Chain Storage)

Public state is declared with `ledger`. All fields initialize to their default values; the constructor can override them.

```compact
export ledger state: State;
export ledger message: Maybe<Opaque<'string'>>;
export ledger sequence: Counter;
export ledger owner: Bytes<32>;
export ledger bidders: Map<Bytes<32>, Uint<16>>;
export sealed ledger maxBids: Uint<16>;
```

[[ledger reference](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#declaring-and-maintaining-public-state)]

### `sealed` Ledger Fields

A `sealed` ledger field can only be written in the **constructor**, never in circuits. This is enforced at compile time. [[sealed ledger](https://github.com/midnightntwrk/midnight-architecture/blob/main/proposals/0009-sealed-ledger-state.md)]

```compact
export sealed ledger maxListSize: Uint<8>;
```

### Supported Ledger-State Types

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `T` (any Compact type) | Simple cell, auto-wrapped as `Cell<T>` |
| `Counter` | Incrementable/decrementable counter |
| `Set<T>` | Unordered set |
| `Map<K, V>` | Key-value map |
| `List<T>` | Ordered list |
| `MerkleTree<n, T>` | Merkle tree (1 < n ≤ 32) |
| `HistoricMerkleTree<n, T>` | Historic Merkle tree |

[[ledger state types](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#ledger-state-types)]

### Reading and Writing Ledger Fields

```compact
// Cell shorthand (write)
F = disclose(x);

// Cell shorthand (read)
return F;

// Counter shorthand
F += 1;   // increment
F -= 1;   // decrement

// Counter method
round.increment(1);

// Map operations
bidders.insert(pubKey, publicBid);
bidders.member(pubKey);       // check membership
bidders.lookup(pubKey);       // get value

// Set operations
organizers.insert(disclose(pubKey));
organizers.member(disclose(pubKey));
```

[[ledger state types](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#ledger-state-types)]

---

## 9. Circuits

Circuits are the primary operational elements — equivalent to functions but compiled into ZK circuits. They **cannot be recursive** (directly or indirectly).

```compact
// Non-exported (private helper)
circuit publicKey(sk: Bytes<32>, sequence: Bytes<32>): Bytes<32> {
  return persistentHash<Vector<3, Bytes<32>>>([pad(32, "bboard:pk:"), sequence, sk]);
}

// Exported (callable entry point)
export circuit post(newMessage: Opaque<'string'>): [] {
  assert(state == State.VACANT, "Attempted to post to an occupied board");
  owner = disclose(publicKey(localSecretKey(), sequence as Field as Bytes<32>));
  message = disclose(some<Opaque<'string'>>(newMessage));
  state = State.OCCUPIED;
}
```

[[circuit definitions](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#circuit-definitions)]

### Pure Circuits

A `pure` circuit has no side effects on ledger state:

```compact
export pure circuit getDappPubKey(_sk: Bytes<32>): Bytes<32> {
  return persistentHash<Vector<2, Bytes<32>>>([pad(32, "silent-auction:pk:"), _sk]);
}
```

---

## 10. Constructor

The constructor runs once when the contract is deployed. It initializes ledger state.

```compact
constructor(_minPrice: Uint<16>, maxBidCount: Uint<16>) {
  const _sk = localSk();
  const pubKey = getDappPubKey(_sk);
  auctionOrganizer = disclose(pubKey);

  maxBids = disclose(maxBidCount);
  hiddenPrice = commitWithSk(_minPrice as Bytes<32>, _sk);
  highestBid = 0;
  auctionState = AuctionState.OPEN;
}
```

[[private reserve auction](https://docs.midnight.network/examples/contracts/private-reserve-auction)]

---

## 11. Witnesses (Private Data)

Witnesses are callback functions declared in Compact but **implemented in TypeScript**. They are the source of private data in a contract.

```compact
// Declaration in Compact
witness localSecretKey(): Bytes<32>;
witness divMod(num1: Uint<16>, num2: Uint<16>): [Uint<16>, Uint<16>];
```

```typescript
// Implementation in TypeScript
export const witnesses = {
  localSecretKey: ({ privateState }: WitnessContext<Ledger, BBoardPrivateState>) =>
    [privateState, privateState.secretKey],
};
```

Any value returned from a witness is considered **private data** and must be handled with `disclose()` before it can be written to the ledger, returned from an exported circuit, or passed to another contract. [[explicit disclosure](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/explicit-disclosure#introduction)]

---

## 12. Explicit Disclosure (`disclose()`)

**Privacy is the default in Compact.** Storing private data in the public ledger, returning it from an exported circuit, or passing it to another contract requires explicit declaration via `disclose()`.

```compact
// WRONG — compiler error
export circuit setf(x: Uint<16>): [] {
  F = x;
}

// CORRECT
export circuit setf(x: Uint<16>): [] {
  F = disclose(x);
}
```

The `disclose()` wrapper has **no runtime effect** — it only tells the compiler that disclosure is intentional. [[explicit disclosure reference](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/compact-reference#explicit-disclosure)]

### Best Practices for `disclose()`

- Place `disclose()` **as close to the disclosure point as possible**.
- When disclosing a hashed value, place `disclose()` **after** the hashing step, not before it.
- For structured values (tuples, vectors, structs), only wrap the portions that contain witness data. [[following indirect disclosures](https://docs.midnight.network/compact/reference/explicit-disclosure#following-indirect-witness-data-assignments)]

---

## 13. Hashing and Commitments

The standard library provides four cryptographic primitives: [[hashes and commitments](https://docs.midnight.network/concepts/how-midnight-works/keeping-data-private#hashes-and-commitments)]

| Function | Output | Persistent? | Disclosure-safe? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `transientHash
